details.instructions:
### Step 1: Read current license state
- MUST Read `Cargo.toml` (root workspace), `crates/core/Cargo.toml`, and `crates/deacon/Cargo.toml`
- MUST Read `LICENSE` file at workspace root
- MUST Grep README.md for any license references

### Step 2: Verify license fields
- MUST verify root `Cargo.toml` line 10 contains `license = "MIT"` under `[workspace.package]`
- MUST verify `crates/core/Cargo.toml` line 5 contains `license.workspace = true`
- MUST verify `crates/deacon/Cargo.toml` line 5 contains `license.workspace = true`
- IF any Cargo.toml has `Apache-2.0` or any license other than MIT, MUST update to MIT
- IF any crate Cargo.toml does NOT inherit from workspace, MUST change to `license.workspace = true`

### Step 3: Verify LICENSE file
- MUST verify `LICENSE` file contains MIT License text
- IF LICENSE contains Apache-2.0 text, MUST replace with MIT License text for copyright holder `get2know.io`

### Step 4: Verify README consistency
- MUST verify README.md license badge references MIT (line ~16: `License-MIT-green.svg`)
- IF badge references Apache-2.0, MUST update to MIT

### Step 5: Verify build
- MUST run `cargo build --quiet` to confirm no metadata errors
- MUST run `cargo metadata --format-version 1` and verify all crates report MIT

acceptance_criteria.text: All Cargo.toml files specify license = MIT (directly or via workspace inheritance)
LICENSE file at workspace root contains MIT text
README.md license badge is consistent with MIT
cargo build succeeds with no metadata warnings

verification: head -1 LICENSE | grep -q 'MIT License' && echo PASS || echo FAIL

details.instructions:
### Step 1: Read current runArgs implementation
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/docker.rs` lines 1748-1770 to confirm runArgs passthrough at line 1756
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/config.rs` and search for `run_args` field definition (around line 520-523) and merge logic (around line 1182)
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/container.rs` to understand how config flows to create_container

### Step 2: Verify Docker mode passthrough
- The runArgs passthrough in `crates/core/src/docker.rs` line 1756 (`args.extend(config.run_args.iter().cloned())`) MUST be present and positioned AFTER Deacon flags (security opts, mounts, env, GPU) and BEFORE the image name argument
- IF runArgs is not in the correct position, MUST move it to after GPU flags and before the image push

### Step 3: Verify Compose mode ignores runArgs
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/compose.rs` method `up_with_injection()` (around lines 301-332)
- MUST verify that `run_args` is NOT passed to any compose command builder
- SHOULD add a tracing warn! log in the compose up path (in `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/compose.rs`) when `config.run_args` is non-empty, warning that runArgs is ignored in Compose mode
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/compose.rs` function `warn_security_options_for_compose()` (around lines 335-370) and add runArgs to the set of warnings if not already present

### Step 4: Add unit tests
- MUST add a test in `crates/core/src/docker.rs` (in the existing `#[cfg(test)]` module) that constructs a DevContainerConfig with run_args containing memory and cpu flags and verifies they appear in the generated docker create command after Deacon flags and before the image name
- MUST add a test verifying empty run_args produces no extra arguments

### Step 5: Build and lint
- MUST run `cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- MUST run `cargo test -p deacon-core`

acceptance_criteria.text: runArgs values from devcontainer config are forwarded to docker create command, positioned after Deacon flags and before image name
runArgs is ignored in Compose mode with a user-visible warning logged
Unit tests verify runArgs passthrough ordering and empty-args behavior
All changes pass cargo clippy and cargo test

details.instructions:
### Step 1: Read current profile infrastructure
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/compose.rs` lines 25-41 (ComposeProject.profiles field)
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/compose.rs` lines 125-156 (ComposeCommand.with_profiles and build_command profile flag injection)
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/compose.rs` lines 660-666 (ComposeManager.get_command threading profiles)
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/compose.rs` lines 600-610 (profile initialization as empty vec)
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/cli.rs` lines 270-300 (Up CLI args area)
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/args.rs` for UpArgs struct
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/compose.rs` lines 40-130 (execute_compose_up)
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/down.rs` lines 330-422 (execute_compose_down)
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/state.rs` lines 39-51 (ComposeState struct)

### Step 2: Add CLI flag for profiles
- MUST add --compose-profile flag to the Up variant in `crates/deacon/src/cli.rs`, positioned near the existing compose-related flags. Use `#[arg(long = "compose-profile")]` with type Vec<String> to support multiple profiles via repeated flags
- MUST add a corresponding compose_profiles: Vec<String> field to UpArgs in `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/args.rs`
- MUST wire the CLI field to UpArgs in the From/mapping implementation

### Step 3: Wire profiles to ComposeProject
- MUST Edit `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/compose.rs` in execute_compose_up() to set `project.profiles = args.compose_profiles.clone()` after project creation (around line 47-50), similar to how project.env_files is set on line 50

### Step 4: Persist profiles in ComposeState
- MUST add `pub profiles: Vec<String>` field to ComposeState struct in `crates/core/src/state.rs` (around line 50)
- MUST update all locations that create ComposeState to include the profiles field
- MUST update execute_compose_down() in `crates/deacon/src/commands/down.rs` line 360 to use `profiles: compose_state.profiles.clone()` instead of `profiles: Vec::new()`

### Step 5: Add Down CLI flag
- SHOULD add --compose-profile flag to the Down variant in `crates/deacon/src/cli.rs` so profiles can be specified during down (needed when state does not have profiles from older state files)
- IF added, MUST wire through to execute_compose_down() and prefer CLI profiles over saved state profiles

### Step 6: Add tests
- MUST add a unit test verifying ComposeCommand with profiles generates correct --profile flags (may already exist at compose.rs:1306-1330, verify it covers multiple profiles)
- MUST add a test that ComposeProject with empty profiles produces no --profile flags
- MUST add a test for CLI arg parsing of --compose-profile dev --compose-profile debug

### Step 7: Build and lint
- MUST run `cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- MUST run `make test-nextest-fast`

acceptance_criteria.text: Compose profiles can be specified via --compose-profile CLI flag and forwarded to docker compose commands
Multiple profiles can be activated simultaneously via repeated --compose-profile flags
docker compose down also receives profile flags from persisted state or CLI override
Default behavior (no profiles) is unchanged - no --profile flags emitted
ComposeState persists profiles for down command reconstruction
All changes pass cargo clippy and cargo test

details.instructions:
### Step 1: Read current implementation state
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/user_mapping.rs` full file. Note the UserMapper trait (lines 177-230), UserMappingService (lines 232-443), mock implementation (lines 517+), and get_host_user_info() (lines 445-515)
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/helpers.rs` lines 121-204. Note that apply_user_mapping() currently only validates/logs but does NOT execute UserMappingService
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/container.rs` lines 470-482. Note T017 TODO and call to apply_user_mapping
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/docker.rs` and search for any existing exec method on the Docker struct for executing commands inside containers
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/config.rs` lines 488-493 for update_remote_user_uid field definition

### Step 2: Create Docker UserMapper implementation
- MUST create a concrete implementation of the UserMapper trait that executes commands inside a running container via docker exec
- The implementation SHOULD live in `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/helpers.rs` or a new sibling file (e.g., docker_user_mapper.rs). Choose based on size
- The struct MUST accept a reference to the container runtime (or docker path string) for executing docker exec commands
- get_current_user() MUST execute `id` inside the container and parse output
- get_user_info() MUST execute `id {username}` or parse /etc/passwd via exec
- update_user_uid() MUST execute `usermod -u {uid} {username}` and `groupmod -g {gid} {group}` inside the container. MUST handle command-not-found gracefully (some minimal images lack usermod)
- create_home_directory() MUST execute `mkdir -p` and `chown` inside the container
- set_workspace_ownership() MUST execute `chown -R {uid}:{gid} {path}` inside the container
- All methods MUST use async tokio::process::Command for docker exec, NOT std::process::Command
- All methods MUST return proper errors via Result, never panic

### Step 3: Wire UserMappingService into apply_user_mapping
- MUST Edit `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/helpers.rs` function apply_user_mapping() (lines 164-188)
- MUST change the function signature to accept a runtime/docker reference parameter for executing commands
- MUST replace the comment-only block (lines 164-188) with actual instantiation and execution: create the Docker UserMapper, create UserMappingService, call service.apply_user_mapping()
- On success, log the result. On failure, log a WARNING but do NOT return Err. Per spec, UID update failure is non-fatal
- MUST update the call site in `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/container.rs` line 481 to pass the runtime reference

### Step 4: Implement skip conditions
- MUST verify UserMappingConfig::needs_uid_mapping() (line 124) returns false when update_remote_user_uid is false
- MUST verify get_host_user_info() returns an error on non-Linux (Windows stub at lines 510-515), causing the skip path in helpers.rs line 146-149
- MUST add a check: IF host UID is 0 (root), skip the update and log why. This MAY be added in UserMappingService::apply_user_mapping() or in helpers.rs
- MUST verify: IF container user UID already matches host UID, the service skips the update (check lines 311 in user_mapping.rs)

### Step 5: Add unit tests
- MUST add tests in `crates/core/src/user_mapping.rs` test module:
  1. Test that root UID (0) is never modified
  2. Test that UID already matching skips update
  3. Test that update_remote_user_uid=false skips all UID work
  4. Test graceful failure handling (mock update_user_uid returning error)
- MUST add a test in helpers for the non-Linux skip path (cfg-gated or with a mock)

### Step 6: Build and lint
- MUST run `cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- MUST run `make test-nextest-fast`

acceptance_criteria.text: updateRemoteUserUID=true updates container user UID/GID to match host on Linux via usermod/groupmod executed in the container
updateRemoteUserUID=false skips the update entirely
Root user (UID 0) is never modified
Non-Linux platforms skip the update entirely (get_host_user_info returns error)
UID already matching skips the update
Failure to update UID logs warning but does not abort up
All changes pass cargo clippy and cargo test with no regressions

details.instructions:
### Step 1: Map the current feature installation code paths
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/features_build.rs` (full file, ~294 lines). This is the BuildKit-based feature build that generates a Dockerfile and runs docker buildx build
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/feature_installer.rs` (full file, ~300 lines). This is the older in-container feature installer
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/container.rs` lines 180-230 to confirm features are built BEFORE container creation
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/mod.rs` lines 190-260 for the overall up flow
- MUST Grep for all imports/usages of feature_installer across the workspace to determine if it is still called anywhere
- MUST Grep for all imports/usages of features_build to confirm it is the active path
- MUST Read `crates/core/src/lib.rs` to check if feature_installer module is still publicly exported
- MUST Read `crates/core/tests/integration_feature_installation.rs` if it exists. Check if tests are ignored

### Step 2: Analyze feature options ENV var handling
- MUST Read features_build.rs and search for how feature options are passed to the Dockerfile
- MUST verify that feature options are set as ENV vars in the generated Dockerfile
- MUST document how the Dockerfile is structured: FROM base, then for each feature a sequence of ENV + COPY + RUN

### Step 3: Analyze cache behavior
- MUST search features_build.rs for cache-related logic (cache-from, cache-to, BuildKit inline cache)
- MUST document whether deterministic layers are achieved (same config = same Dockerfile = cache hit)

### Step 4: Identify the no-features path
- MUST confirm that container.rs lines 193-222 correctly skip feature build when config.features is empty or None

### Step 5: Document findings
- MUST Write findings to `.maverick/context/feature-install-timing-research.md` with sections:
  1. Current State: Feature installation already happens at build time via features_build.rs
  2. Orphaned Code: feature_installer.rs is unused. List all references and whether they can be removed
  3. Feature Options: How ENV vars are set in the generated Dockerfile
  4. Cache Behavior: Whether deterministic layers are achieved
  5. No-Features Path: Confirmation that it is correctly handled
  6. Gaps Found: Any remaining issues
  7. Recommended Changes: Specific actions for the implementation bead

### Step 6: Verify research completeness
- The output document MUST be actionable. The implementation bead agent should be able to read it and know exactly what to do
- IF the research finds that feature installation timing is ALREADY CORRECT, document this clearly and recommend the implementation bead focus on cleanup and adding/improving tests

acceptance_criteria.text: Research document maps all feature installation code paths with file:line references
Document identifies whether feature_installer.rs is orphaned or still used
Document describes feature options ENV var handling in generated Dockerfile
Document confirms or identifies gaps in the no-features skip path
Document provides actionable recommendations for the implementation bead

details.instructions:
### Step 0: Read research findings
- MUST Read `.maverick/context/feature-install-timing-research.md` produced by the research bead
- Use findings to guide all subsequent steps. The research document is authoritative for what needs to change

### Step 1: Verify current build-phase installation
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/features_build.rs` to confirm BuildKit-based feature building is working
- MUST Read `crates/deacon/src/commands/up/container.rs` lines 180-230 to confirm features are built before container creation
- IF research found that feature installation is ALREADY in build phase (likely), proceed to cleanup and testing

### Step 2: Clean up orphaned in-container installer
- IF `crates/core/src/feature_installer.rs` is confirmed unused by the research:
  - SHOULD remove or mark as deprecated the unused FeatureInstaller struct and its methods
  - MUST NOT break any compilation. Check all imports first via Grep
  - IF `crates/core/src/lib.rs` exports feature_installer module, SHOULD remove the export only if no downstream code uses it
  - IF integration tests reference it, MUST either remove the ignored tests or update them to test the new path
- IF research found feature_installer IS still used somewhere, MUST refactor that usage to go through features_build.rs instead

### Step 3: Verify feature options as ENV vars
- MUST Read features_build.rs Dockerfile generation logic
- MUST verify each feature options are emitted as ENV KEY=VALUE lines before the feature RUN install.sh
- IF options are not properly passed as ENV vars, MUST fix the Dockerfile generation

### Step 4: Verify deterministic layers for cache
- MUST verify that the same config produces the same Dockerfile content (deterministic ordering)
- MUST verify that features are ordered by dependency resolution, not random iteration
- IF feature ordering is non-deterministic, MUST switch to deterministic ordering

### Step 5: Verify no-features skip path
- MUST confirm container.rs lines 193-222 skip the feature build when config has no features
- MUST add a test if one does not exist: config with empty/null features should not trigger any build

### Step 6: Add or improve tests
- MUST add a unit test verifying:
  1. Generated Dockerfile contains correct FROM, ENV, COPY, RUN lines for a sample feature
  2. Feature options are passed as ENV vars
  3. Empty features config produces no Dockerfile/build
  4. Multiple features produce deterministic ordering
- IF docker integration tests exist but are ignored, evaluate whether they should be enabled or replaced
- MUST configure any new integration tests in `.config/nextest.toml` with appropriate test groups

### Step 7: Build and lint
- MUST run `cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- MUST run `make test-nextest-fast`

acceptance_criteria.text: Features are installed during image build phase (BuildKit), not in running container, confirmed by code path and tests
Feature options are passed correctly as ENV vars in the generated Dockerfile
Configs without features skip the feature build step entirely
Rebuilding without config changes uses Docker cache (deterministic Dockerfile generation)
Orphaned in-container feature installer code is cleaned up or removed
All changes pass cargo clippy and cargo test with no regressions
New integration tests are configured in nextest.toml with appropriate groups
